assumptions: The developer has a supported local machine and administrator rights when the chosen installer requires them.
No Docker Desktop install, Docker Hub account, or welcome container exists yet.

developer_goal: Reach Docker's documented first running container success from a fresh developer setup, including account creation if the selected route requires it.

first_success.outcome: The official welcome container is running and its frontend is reachable on localhost:8080.
first_success.observable_signal: http://localhost:8080 renders the welcome container landing page, and Docker Desktop Containers shows the running container.

route_selection.rule: Prefer Docker Desktop Get Started over Engine-only installs when Desktop is documented as the all-in-one package, but resolve OS installer path and whether Docker Hub signup is in the required path before counting.

candidates.name: Docker Desktop on Mac, then docker/welcome-to-docker
candidates.reason: Fully documented install and first-container steps, but Mac Apple silicon versus Intel installers differ and Desktop sign-in is recommended rather than required for the welcome container.

candidates.name: Docker Desktop on Windows or Linux, then the same welcome container
candidates.reason: Peer OS install guides change required system steps, privileges, and dependency setup such as Linux pass initialization for sign-in.

candidates.name: Docker Engine without Desktop
candidates.reason: Relevant for server-oriented participants, but it is a different install surface from the Desktop Get Started series.

candidates.name: Create a Docker Hub account during or before Desktop use
candidates.reason: Hub signup fields are publicly visible, yet the Get Started first-container path does not require sign-in; large-organization commercial use may require a paid subscription entitlement.

required_path.action: Create a Docker Hub account if the selected route treats sign-in or Hub identity as required.

required_fields.label: Subscription Service Agreement, Privacy Policy, and Data Processing Terms

required_path.observable_result: The developer has a Docker ID that can sign in to Docker Desktop or Hub.

required_path.action: Download and install Docker Desktop for the selected operating system and accept the Subscription Service Agreement.

required_fields.label: Operating system and architecture installer choice

required_path.observable_result: Docker Desktop is installed and finishes initialization.

required_path.action: Run the welcome container and open its frontend.
required_path.observable_result: localhost:8080 serves the welcome page and Desktop lists the running container.

prerequisites.description: A supported OS release and enough local resources for Docker Desktop, such as at least 4 GB RAM on Mac.

external_gates.description: Commercial use in organizations above Docker's stated employee or revenue thresholds requires a paid Docker Desktop subscription.

unavoidable_waits.description: Docker Desktop must finish initialization before docker run can succeed.
The first run must pull the docker/welcome-to-docker image before the container serves traffic.

platform_outcomes.description: Docker Desktop starts the Engine and reports ready after install.
The welcome container listens on port 8080 and appears in Desktop Containers.

excluded.reason: Sign-in docs present benefits and recommendations; the Get Started welcome-container path does not require it.

excluded.item: Building and publishing a custom image to Docker Hub
excluded.reason: Later chapters in the introduction series, after run your first container.

sources.title: Introduction series
evidence_supported: Install, first container, build, and publish sequence

sources.title: Install Docker Desktop on Windows
evidence_supported: Windows as a peer Desktop install path

sources.title: Install Docker Desktop on Linux
evidence_supported: Linux as a peer Desktop install path

sources.title: Sign in to Docker Desktop
evidence_supported: Not required for the welcome-container command itself

uncertainties.question: Which OS installer defines the Desktop path, and is Docker Hub account creation part of the required journey to first container?
uncertainties.impact: OS choice changes install actions; including Hub signup adds fields the Get Started container path does not require, while excluding it conflicts with the account-creation audit boundary.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A workshop OS choice plus an explicit rule on whether Desktop sign-in or Hub signup is in-path before docker run.

uncertainties.question: Does the participant's organization require a paid Docker Desktop subscription before Accept and run are lawful?
uncertainties.impact: A paid entitlement gate can block the otherwise free personal or small-business Desktop path.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Participant organization size and revenue relative to Docker's commercial-use thresholds.
